evals.prompt: Here is our Trial Balance. Can you prepare the P&L and Balance Sheet? We also need to record $5k in depreciation.
evals.expected_output: Should validate the Trial Balance (Debits=Credits). Should record the $5k adjusting entry for depreciation. Should map accounts to the Income Statement and Balance Sheet. Should ensure Net Income flows into Retained Earnings. Should provide the full Financial Statement Package.
assertions: Records the $5k adjusting entry for depreciation
Correctly maps accounts to P&L and BS
Output follows Financial Statement Package structure

evals.prompt: Prepare the year-end statements knowing we breached a loan covenant in November and got a waiver in February, after year-end but before issuing. Current or non-current debt?
evals.expected_output: Under IAS 1, classification reflects conditions AT the reporting date: waiver received after year-end means the liability was current at year-end (no unconditional right to defer) - classify current, disclose the subsequent-event waiver. (US GAAP differs - waiver before issuance can support non-current.)
assertions: Classifies the debt as current under IFRS reasoning
Discloses the waiver as a non-adjusting subsequent event

evals.prompt: Our draft balance sheet shows negative cash of -45M because of outstanding checks. Present it as negative cash?
evals.expected_output: No - a book overdraft from outstanding checks is reclassified to accounts payable (or bank overdraft liability if a true bank overdraft). Cash can't be negative on the face; also check offsetting rules across accounts at the same bank.
assertions: Reclassifies the book overdraft to liabilities
Distinguishes book overdraft from bank overdraft
Considers legal right of offset before netting accounts
Corrects the cash flow statement presentation accordingly

evals.prompt: Prepare an indirect-method cash flow statement from the attached movement data (evals/files/movement_data.csv): CFO with working-capital adjustments, CFI, CFF, and prove closing cash.
assertions: Subtracts AR and inventory increases, adds payables increase
